ksa_text: Knowledge of data privacy and protection techniques as applied to data architecture, including data masking, tokenization, anonymization, pseudonymization, and privacy-by-design architectural patterns.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Data Architecture & Analytics KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.445) to justify inclusion for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: This Knowledge KSA was excluded as an edge case, yet it has a composite score of 0.445 (moderate signal). Knowledge-dimension KSAs represent conceptual awareness rather than execution; exclusion may be too aggressive.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Edge-domain Knowledge KSAs require more than moderate signal to justify inclusion; the role's scope does not regularly engage with Data Architecture & Analytics. Exclusion upheld.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: Upheld: edge-domain exclusion maintained. Data Architecture & Analytics is not sufficiently central to the Chief of Staff to CDAIO to warrant inclusion at this signal level.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Chief of Staff to CDAIO roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Data Architecture & Analytics is not a standard competency expectation for Chief of Staff to CDAIO. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the Chief of Staff to CDAIO competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.445) and edge-domain placement (Data Architecture & Analytics) do not meet inclusion threshold for the Chief of Staff to CDAIO. All passes support exclusion.
